events.description: The first confirmed case of mpox in the United States is reported to the CDC in a Massachusetts resident who had recently traveled from Canada.
The CDC officially activates its emergency outbreak response to address the emerging mpox situation.
The World Health Organization (WHO) declares the multi-country mpox outbreak a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC).
New York Governor Kathy Hochul declares a state disaster emergency to mobilize resources for the mpox outbreak.
California Governor Gavin Newsom proclaims a State of Emergency to bolster vaccination efforts and coordinate the state's response.
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) officially declares the mpox outbreak a national Public Health Emergency.

outcome_description: The 2022 mpox outbreak in the U.S. was managed through a coordinated federal and state public health response, including the declaration of a national public health emergency and a targeted vaccination campaign that successfully contained the spread. Public opinion was characterized by initial concern followed by a shift toward reliance on targeted public health messaging and vaccination efforts within affected communities.
outcome_source: Kaiser Family Foundation (KFF) COVID-19 Vaccine Monitor (August 2022 survey regarding public concern and awareness of mpox)
